dialogos.texto_en: Mmmmm, should I swim a little more?\nThere's still time, right?
Didn't you swim enough for now, after swimming the WHOLE\nday?
It just feels so good, you know?\nThe ocean is so beautiful, and the more I swim,\nthe more I feel like it's not enough!
Come on, Nino, let's swim together!
I'm good. Right now, I feel like\njust gazing at the ocean.
Yotsuba and Nino, huh?\nWhat are you guys up to?
Ah, Futaro.
Can't you say something?\nYotsuba is about to go swimming AGAIN.
Well, I want to swim as much as I can! \nUesugi-san, you agree, right?
Yeah, for sure.\nI understand the feeling.
But take a little break, too.\nWhen you're swimming so much it's easy to\nget dehydrated.
Well, if you put it that way, gazing at the ocean\ndoesn't sound that bad, right?
Just relaxing, huh.
Hmm, I guess it's weird to\npush yourself too hard during a vacation.
I think about that sometimes.\nNowadays vacations are always about going \naway somewhere, ya know?
I mean, that's true, but%p-2;――
I think that just being with the people you love is a great way \nto spend your vacation.
I see.\nWell, I guess I'm disturbing you guys.
N-no, you're not interrupting.\nWe came as a group to Okinawa!
Teehee, that's right.
W-well, I guess everyone has their own way\nof spending their vacation. Some people relax at home,\nsome people go on vacation, and some people study.
Studying is definitely not an option, though.
Oh, speaking of relaxing,\nthere's a word in Okinawa called 'uchina time.'
Huh? What's that?
It sounds like a secret technique!
What the? A secret technique?
It's like as if you're\nmanipulating the flow of time.
Secret Technique: Uchina Time!\nNow we control the time!!
...You're the only one who thinks that.
Really?\nI thought it sounded kind of cool.\nWhat do you think, Nino?
Don't drag me into this.
AHH!
Uchina time refers to the unique sense of time\nin Okinawa.
A sense of time?
Yotsuba, your imagination runs wild...\nThat's not an ordinary thought that comes to mind.
Yay! I got a compliment!
Yeah, it kinda sounded like a compliment?\nIt's an idea I couldn't come up with.
Uchina time refers to the unique sense of time\nin Okinawa.
A sense of time?
You're kinda right.
Really?!
Huh? What do you mean?\nHave you just said that I'm correct, Futaro?
I'm not kidding.\nWell, it's a unique Okinawan culture.\nIt's not your fault if you guys don't know about it.
Uchina time refers to the unique sense of time\nin Okinawa.
A sense of time?
For example, if we made plans to meet somewhere,\nwe would set up a time and meet accordingly%p-2;――
In Okinawa, it's common to arrive late for a meeting,\nand the other person won't be complaining.
Huh? Is that true?
Of course, not all Okinawans are like that.\nIn the end, it probably depends\non the individual's personality.
So, Uchina time means being loose with time?
...Yeah, you could say that.
If you're that loose with time,\nwouldn't you have trouble catching a train,\nfor example?
Well, there's only one monorail in Okinawa,\nso maybe that's a part of their unique Okinawan identity.
Also, when it comes to school or work,\nit might be tough if you go by Uchina time, right?
Apparently, they are punctual in a professionnal setting, \nbut tardy in their private lives.
Hahaha, that's true.\nThe staff of the hotel restaurant was always on time.
But it's an interesting lifestyle.\nFor people who live life at their own pace,\nOkinawa might be a really nice place.
When traveling, you're sometimes surprised\nby these unique cultural differences.
It's the same overseas, isn't it?\nI often hear that Italy's sense of time differs from that of \nother European countries.
Oh yeah, I've heard that too.\nTheir sense of punctuality is so different from ours it'll \ndefinitely be causing problems.
Did you know that Greeks live at their own pace too?\nMaybe they'd get along well with Okinawans.
Oh really, I didn't know that.
...They probably have different values, right?
It's interesting how people\nare changed by the culture they are living in. \nIt's fascinating.
